mnemonics: INDEX F.A.M. (Functionality, Alignment, MultiIndex) - Other functionalities on indexes are like a helpful family that keeps your data aligned, even when it's complex and multi-level.
keyPoints: Index objects can be reset, turning index levels into regular columns with `reset_index()`.
Indexes can be set from existing columns to create a new meaningful order with `set_index()`.
MultiIndex (hierarchical index) objects allow for higher-dimensional data representation in a 2D DataFrame structure.
commonMistakes: Accidentally dropping the index when you meant to reset it by using `inplace=False` and not assigning the result to a variable.
Setting an index on a non-unique column, which can lead to unexpected behavior when selecting data.
Forgetting that `reset_index()` will create a column for *each* level when used on a MultiIndex DataFrame, potentially creating many new columns.
